sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Ionis' Dawnzera Gets EU Approval for Hereditary Angioedema
feed.summary: Ionis Pharmaceuticals and its partner Otsuka Pharmaceutical received European Commission approval for Dawnzera (donidalorsen) to prevent hereditary angioedema (HAE) attacks in adults and adolescents. This approval follows positive data from phase III OASIS-HAE and OASISplus studies, which demonstrated significant reductions in HAE attack rates. Ionis will receive a $15 million milestone payment and tiered royalties from Otsuka for the EU approval.

feed.title: Progressive’s Quigg sells $336k in shares
feed.summary: Progressive Corp's Chief Strategy Officer, Andrew J. Quigg, sold 1,649 shares of company stock for a total of $336,973 on January 21, 2026, under a pre-arranged trading plan. Following this transaction, Quigg directly owns 39,626.185 shares. The article also mentions recent company news including a new pet insurance offering and varying analyst ratings from Barclays, BMO Capital, and Wells Fargo.

feed.title: Trump sues JPMorgan Chase, Jamie Dimon for $5B over alleged ‘debanking’
feed.summary: Donald Trump has filed a $5 billion lawsuit against JPMorgan Chase and its CEO, Jamie Dimon, alleging that the bank "debanked" him by closing his accounts in 2021 due to political motivations. Trump claims the bank's decision caused him significant financial and reputational harm, while JPMorgan Chase maintains it closes accounts due to legal or regulatory risk, not political or religious reasons. This lawsuit follows Trump's public statements on discrimination against conservatives by big banks and his recent threats of legal action against the bank.

feed.title: Inspire Medical (INSP) Faces Medicare Reimbursement Adjustment
feed.summary: Inspire Medical (INSP) has received clarification regarding Medicare reimbursement policies for its procedures, resolving prior uncertainties about ICD-10 code eligibility. While this adjustment means no uplift in reimbursement for certain sleep apnea procedures, Evercore ISI maintains an Outperform rating with a $125 price target, anticipating stability for the company. Inspire Medical Systems, known for its minimally invasive sleep apnea solutions, demonstrates strong financial health despite the policy change.

feed.title: Is It Too Late To Consider Applied Materials (AMAT) After 72% One Year Rally?
feed.summary: Applied Materials (AMAT) has rallied 72% over the past year, leading investors to question its current valuation of US$325.24. According to Simply Wall St's analysis, both a Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) model and a Price-to-Earnings (P/E) ratio comparison suggest the stock is overvalued. The DCF analysis estimates an intrinsic value of $139.91, indicating a 132.5% overvaluation, while its P/E of 36.9x is above Simply Wall St's "Fair Ratio" of 32.1x.

feed.title: Regent Seven Seas Cruises Rolls Out Casual Evening Dining Option
feed.summary: Regent Seven Seas Cruises is introducing a new casual evening dining option across its fleet. The Pool Grill will offer grill items and a new gourmet pizzeria menu from 6 p.m. to 9 p.m., featuring dishes like strip loin minute steak, lamb chops, and various gourmet pizzas. This initiative, praised by Chief Luxury Officer Jason Montague, aims to provide guests with a more relaxed yet high-quality dining experience.

feed.title: Valero and Phillips 66 Buy Venezuelan Crude in Early U.S. Deal
feed.summary: Valero and Phillips 66 have become the first U.S. refiners to purchase Venezuelan crude under a new agreement between Washington and Caracas, signaling a potential reopening of oil supply flows. The crude, bought from trading house Vitol, was priced at a significant discount to Brent. These deals mark a shift towards direct purchases from trading houses, diverging from previous arrangements made through Chevron, and represent an early step in potentially restoring pre-sanction supply relationships.

feed.title: Barclays Raises Evergy (EVRG) Price Target to $82.00 | EVRG Stoc
feed.summary: Barclays has raised its price target for Evergy (EVRG) to $82.00 from $80.00, maintaining an "Overweight" rating. This adjustment reflects a 2.50% increase in their price outlook for the regulated electric utility company serving eastern Kansas and western Missouri. The average one-year price target from 11 analysts is $84.09, suggesting a 9.61% upside from the current price, while GuruFocus' GF Value estimates a 14.99% downside.

feed.title: Consolidated Edison (ED) Maintains Underweight Rating by Barclays | ED Stock News
feed.summary: Barclays has reiterated its 'Underweight' rating for Consolidated Edison (ED) while slightly lowering its price target to $100.00. This follows several other analysts' mixed but cautious ratings and price target adjustments for the utility company. The average analyst price target for ED is $104.25, with a consensus "Hold" recommendation, while GuruFocus estimates a slight downside from its current price.

feed.title: Why Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings (NCLH) Is Down 9.6% After EU–U.S. Greenland Tension Jolts Travel Sentiment
feed.summary: Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings (NCLH) stock dropped 9.6% due to heightened geopolitical tensions between the EU and the U.S. over Greenland, impacting travel sentiment. While the selloff appears to be a sentiment shock rather than a change in fundamental catalysts, such external events amplify concerns about the company's debt and volatility. Investors are encouraged to consider multiple perspectives on NCLH's fair value and its underlying financial health.

feed.title: Alexandria Real Estate Equities (ARE) Valuation Reconsidered As Life Science REIT Rerating Gains Traction
feed.summary: Alexandria Real Estate Equities (ARE) is being re-evaluated as a specialized life science REIT rather than a generic office REIT, leading to renewed share price momentum. Despite a sharp short-term rebound, various valuation metrics, including a Price-to-Sales ratio of 3.3x and a DCF model, suggest that ARE shares, currently at US$58.45, may still be undervalued compared to both peers and internal fair value estimates. Investors are encouraged to consider the company's risks and perform their own research.

feed.title: Advanced Micro Devices’ Winning Streak Continues. How High Can the Chip Stock Go?
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) stock continues its rally, outperforming the semiconductor industry over the past year. Analysts believe this impressive run is likely to continue, prompting questions about how much higher the chip stock can climb. The article highlights AMD's strong performance within the semiconductor sector.

feed.title: Tesla Starts Public Robotaxi Rides in Austin With Ex-Employee
feed.summary: Tesla has commenced public robotaxi rides in Austin, Texas, with its first customer experiencing a ride without any human intervention in the front seats. This marks a significant milestone for the company, which has been conducting human-supervised rides for several months. CEO Elon Musk highlighted this progress on X, sharing a video from a former Tesla AI engineer who was reportedly the first public user.

feed.title: Your Con Ed Bill Just Went Up, New York: State Approves Hike
feed.summary: The New York State Public Service Commission has approved rate increases for Con Edison customers, leading to approximately a 9% hike for electricity and a 6% hike for gas over three years. This will result in higher monthly bills for 4.5 million customers in New York City and Westchester, aiming to cover increasing costs, system investments, and programs related to energy efficiency and affordability. The approved hikes are significantly lower than Con Ed's initial proposal and include measures for greater transparency and customer support.

feed.title: Phillips 66 Wood River Refinery Invests Over $1.3 Million in Local Communities in 2025
feed.summary: In 2025, the Phillips 66 Wood River Refinery invested over $1.3 million in the Metro East region through corporate contributions, employee giving, and volunteer programs. This includes direct contributions, funds raised via the United Way, and significant amounts through volunteer and matching gift programs, supporting over 100 regional organizations. The refinery prioritizes education, environmental sustainability, community safety, and social advancement, with employees contributing thousands of volunteer hours to various causes.

feed.title: PNC simplifies investing for wealth management customers
feed.summary: PNC Wealth Management has launched a new digital brokerage account opening process, allowing customers to open and fund brokerage accounts entirely online. This initiative is part of a broader digital transformation by the Pittsburgh-based bank, which is increasing its technology spending and integrating new customers from a recent acquisition.

feed.title: Trump’s retribution campaign hits JPMorgan Chase — with $5 billion on the line
feed.summary: Donald Trump has filed a lawsuit against JPMorgan Chase and its CEO Jamie Dimon, seeking at least $5 billion in damages. Trump alleges the bank closed his accounts for political reasons in late 2021 after the January 6th insurrection, accusing them of "debanking" him based on "woke beliefs." JPMorgan Chase has stated the lawsuit is meritless and that accounts are closed due to legal or regulatory risk, not political or religious reasons.

feed.title: Trump sues Dimon and JPMorgan Chase
feed.summary: Donald Trump has filed a $5 billion lawsuit against JPMorgan Chase and its CEO, Jamie Dimon, alleging "de-banking" after his accounts were closed or restricted following the January 6th Capitol insurrection. The bank stated it closes accounts due to legal or regulatory risk, not political reasons, and will defend itself in court. This lawsuit comes amidst a broader debate in Congress and on Wall Street regarding banks restricting accounts for non-financial reasons.

feed.title: Live Oak Bancshares (LOB) Reports $44M Net Income in Q4 2025 with 17% Loan Growth
feed.summary: Live Oak Bancshares (LOB) reported strong financial results for Q4 2025, with a net income of $44 million and earnings per share of $0.95, tripling Q4 2024 figures. The company achieved $6.2 billion in loan production, marking a 17% year-over-year growth, and saw a significant increase in customer relationship expansion. Management plans to continue investing in technology and AI to enhance operational efficiency and customer service.

feed.title: ADM Named to FORTUNE Magazine’s 2026 Most Admired Company List
feed.summary: ADM (NYSE: ADM) has been recognized by FORTUNE Magazine as one of the world's most admired companies in the food production industry for 2026. This prestigious list, developed by FORTUNE and Korn Ferry, evaluates corporate reputation based on nine criteria including investment value, quality of management and products, social responsibility, and ability to attract talent. The company's Chair and CEO, Juan Luciano, expressed pride in the team's dedication and innovation, highlighting ADM's commitment to leveraging nature's power to improve lives globally and advance its strategy.

feed.title: Saba Capital sold BlackRock ESG (ECAT) shares worth $4.19 million
feed.summary: Saba Capital Management, a ten-percent owner of BlackRock ESG Capital Allocation Term Trust (NASDAQ:ECAT), sold 272,613 shares of common stock across two transactions on January 20 and 21, 2026. These sales totaled $4,193,816 at prices between $15.38 and $15.39 per share. Despite these sales, ECAT maintains a 21.57% dividend yield and has shown a 13.96% total return over the past year.

feed.title: Progressive corp (PGR) CIO Bauer sells $634,506 in shares
feed.summary: Progressive Corp's Chief Investment Officer, Jonathan S. Bauer, sold 3,105 shares of common stock for $634,506 on January 21, 2026, under a pre-arranged trading plan. This transaction leaves him with 26,248.8 directly owned shares and an additional 122.557 shares indirectly through a 401(k) plan. The article also notes recent company developments, including a new pet insurance offering and mixed analyst ratings on Progressive's stock.

feed.title: Why Arista Networks Stock Popped Today
feed.summary: Shares of Arista Networks (NYSE: ANET) rose by over 10% today, following BNP Paribas naming it a top AI stock for 2026. This surge, accompanied by high trading volume, indicates strong investor interest in the company's role in the AI sector, including its partnership with AI start-up Anthropic. The exact catalyst for today's jump is not fully clear, but it likely reflects investors positioning for long-term gains in AI backend networking demand.

feed.title: Freight Technologies Launches Zayren Pro with Next-Generation AI Agents
feed.summary: Freight Technologies (Fr8Tech) has launched Zayren Pro, a premium commercial version of its AI-driven logistics platform, designed to expand its subscription-based SaaS revenue. Zayren Pro moves beyond simple rate prediction to enable actionable procurement execution, offering advanced AI capabilities, unlimited query capacity, and a proprietary carrier portal for the U.S.–Mexico nearshoring corridor. This platform empowers shippers and brokers with smart procurement tools and direct execution capabilities, connecting them with verified carriers.

feed.title: BlackRock CEO Larry Fink: 'What happens to everyone else' if AI fuels inequality?
feed.summary: BlackRock CEO Larry Fink expressed concerns about artificial intelligence's potential to exacerbate societal inequality, questioning the implications for those left behind by the technology's rapid advancement. He emphasized the urgent need for leaders to consider AI's broader impact beyond productivity and profits. Fink also highlighted the importance of a well-compensated workforce to combat the rise of populism.

feed.title: Analyst Remains Bullish on Amazon, TD Cowen Raises PT From $300 to $315
feed.summary: TD Cowen has raised its price target for Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) from $300 to $315, maintaining a Buy rating, based on a positive outlook from their annual advertising buyer survey. Concurrently, Raymond James analyst Josh Beck also expects a strong Q4 2025 for Amazon, driven by holiday trends, ad checks, and AWS performance, highlighting AI as a major catalyst for the stock in 2026. The article notes Amazon's significant position in e-commerce and cloud services but suggests other AI stocks might offer greater upside with less risk.

feed.title: Lansing Coca-Cola distributor announces layoffs. What we know
feed.summary: Great Lakes Coca-Cola Distribution, a Lansing-area distributor, announced it will lay off 62 workers, including CDL drivers and warehouse staff, by April 3. Additionally, 99 employees, such as merchandisers and sales account managers, will be reassigned. The notice from the company, owned by Reyes Holdings LLC, did not specify the future of the Lansing location or the new work locations for reassigned staff.

feed.title: Novartis Wins Bid to Shut Down MSN Challenge of Entresto Patent
feed.summary: A federal judge has ruled in favor of Novartis, preventing MSN Laboratories from reopening patent infringement disputes concerning Novartis's heart-failure drug, Entresto. The decision from the US District Court for the District of Delaware asserts that questions regarding the patent's scope, validity, and infringement that Novartis alleged against MSN were already fully decided in a prior 2020 Hatch-Waxman case. This ruling blocks MSN's attempt to challenge US Patent No. 8,101,659 after importing and offering a generic version of Entresto.

feed.title: Autodesk layoffs today: Software company cuts 7% of jobs in latest tech downsizing
feed.summary: Autodesk announced plans to lay off about 1,000 employees, primarily in sales roles, representing approximately 7% of its workforce. This move is part of a multi-phase restructuring plan to reallocate resources towards strategic priorities. CEO Andrew Anagnost stated that these are not annual layoffs or a shift to AI replacement, and the company expects pre-tax restructuring charges between $135 and $160 million.

feed.title: Coca-Cola vs. Monster Beverage: Which Stock Stays Ahead of the Curve?
feed.summary: This article compares Coca-Cola (KO) and Monster Beverage (MNST), highlighting their different market strategies and investment cases. While Coca-Cola offers defensive stability with broad market share and diversification, Monster Beverage provides growth-oriented exposure through its dominance in the fast-growing energy drink market. The analysis suggests Monster Beverage is the more attractive choice for performance-driven returns due to its stronger stock performance, solid growth trajectory, and positive estimate revisions.

feed.title: Albemarle shares jump as rising lithium prices build upon efficiency gains
feed.summary: Albemarle Corp. shares have risen over 30% this year, driven by analyst predictions of increasing lithium prices and successful efficiency initiatives. Analysts from HSBC and Truist have upgraded their ratings and significantly raised price targets, reflecting optimism about Albemarle's prospects in the rebounding lithium market. The company is set to report Q4 and full-year results soon, with expectations of narrowing losses and increased revenue, supported by reduced capital spending and asset sales.

feed.title: Mixed options sentiment in Alphabet Class A with shares up 1.07%
feed.summary: Alphabet Class A (GOOGL) shares rose by 1.07% to $331.91, exhibiting mixed options sentiment. While overall options volume was average, calls outnumbered puts, and put-call skew flattened, suggesting a modestly bullish outlook. The company is set to report earnings on February 4, 2026, with option markets pricing in a 50% probability of a move greater than 5.1%.

feed.title: Is GM Set for 14th Straight EPS Beat in Q4? How to Play the Stock Now
feed.summary: General Motors (GM) is predicted by Zacks Investment Research to achieve its 14th consecutive earnings beat in Q4 2025, driven by strong full-year sales and strategic adjustments in its EV and China operations. Despite a decline in Q4 US sales and EV sales, GM emerged as the top-selling automaker in the US for 2025 and is showing strong profitability in China. The company's stock is considered undervalued and a compelling buy due to improved fundamentals, a shift to higher-margin vehicles, and growth in its software and services business.

feed.title: BMS bets on Janux’s novel solid tumour therapy through $850m deal
feed.summary: Bristol Myers Squibb (BMS) has entered into an exclusive global licensing agreement with Janux Therapeutics for a novel solid tumor therapy, valued at up to $850 million. This deal strengthens BMS's oncology pipeline as it faces upcoming patent expirations for key drugs like Opdivo and Eliquis. Janux will handle preclinical development up to IND submission, after which BMS will take over clinical development and commercialization, with Janux also receiving tiered royalties on global sales.

feed.title: Final Trades: Vertex Pharma, Kimberly-Clark, S&P Global and Exxon Mobil
feed.summary: The Investment Committee has revealed their top stock picks for the second half of the year. The report focuses on companies such as Vertex Pharma, Kimberly-Clark, S&P Global, and Exxon Mobil, providing viewers with stocks to watch. The article is primarily a video segment summary, encouraging viewers to watch the full discussion.

feed.title: Tech briefs: Deere's enhanced AI integration; new drone-based crop intel project launched
feed.summary: This article provides tech briefs on agricultural innovations. It covers Deere's advanced AI integration in its operations and the launch of a new drone-based crop intelligence project.

feed.title: Colgate-Palmolive & WHO Foundation: Oral Health Education
feed.summary: Colgate-Palmolive has partnered with the WHO Foundation in a multi-year funding agreement to advance oral health education and awareness. This collaboration aims to elevate oral health as a public health priority, support its integration into national health systems, and expand preventive measures. The initiative, announced at WEF's Annual Meeting, focuses on scaling education, guiding policy, and increasing prioritization of oral health globally.

feed.title: Tractor Supply plans new Plymouth Township location
feed.summary: Tractor Supply Company is planning a new retail store in Plymouth Township, Michigan, near a new Meijer grocery store. The township's planning commission has approved the special land use request for the 21,533-square-foot building and outdoor display area. This development is part of a growing commercial cluster along the Five Mile corridor, and commissioners emphasized the importance of preserving heritage trees and ensuring pedestrian connectivity.

feed.title: Lemonade’s Tesla insurance product validates FSD technology, Morgan Stanley notes
feed.summary: Morgan Stanley reiterated an Equalweight rating and $425.00 price target for Tesla, noting that Lemonade's new autonomous car insurance product validates Tesla's Full Self-Driving (FSD) technology. Lemonade's policy ties insurance pricing to FSD miles driven, offering up to 50% rate reductions, which incentivizes FSD usage and signals the insurer's recognition of improved accident risk during autonomous operation. This development suggests that insurance could become a market-based validator for autonomous driving advancements.

feed.title: Healthcare debate should focus on out-of-pocket costs, not list prices – Novartis CEO (NVS:NYSE)
feed.summary: Novartis CEO Vas Narasimhan argues that the U.S. healthcare debate should prioritize reducing patient out-of-pocket costs rather than focusing on drug list prices. He suggests that current policies, like the push for G7-comparable drug pricing, could delay the launch of new medicines in Europe and discourage pharmaceutical innovation. Narasimhan also noted that most U.S. healthcare spending is not on drugs, but on other areas like hospitals and waste.

feed.title: Josh Brown's "best stocks in the market:" First Solar
feed.summary: Josh Brown, CEO of Ritholtz Wealth Management, appeared on CNBC's "Halftime Report" to discuss his "best stocks in the market." He specifically spotlighted First Solar (FSLR) during his segment. The article provides only this brief mention and does not offer further details from his analysis.

feed.title: Target Stacks Its Board With Star Executives From Nike, HanesBrands
feed.summary: Target Corporation has appointed John Hoke, III, a longtime Nike design leader, and Steve Bratspies, former CEO of HanesBrands, to its board of directors. These appointments are part of Target's strategy to enhance innovation and increase shareholder value as it enters a new growth phase with CEO Michael Fiddelke. The company also declared a quarterly dividend of $1.14 per common share, payable on March 1, 2026.

feed.title: Smart Money Is Betting Big In CSGP Options
feed.summary: Benzinga's options scanner detected over 12 options trades for CoStar Group (NASDAQ: CSGP) totaling $1,473,080, with significant investors targeting a price range of $40.0 to $95.0 over the next three months. The stock is currently trading at $67.55, up 4.29%, with analysts having an average target price of $72.0.

feed.title: A Closer Look at UnitedHealth Group's Options Market Dynamics
feed.summary: Financial giants have shown significant bullish interest in UnitedHealth Group (NYSE: UNH) through the options market, with 91 unusual trades observed. While 47% of traders were bullish and 29% bearish, call options accounted for a higher value ($4,094,690) compared to puts ($2,405,589). The analysis indicates a targeted price range of $200.0 to $560.0 for UNH during the last quarter, with current trading showing a 1.82% price increase to $354.07 and the stock potentially approaching overbought conditions.

feed.title: Intel, GE Aerospace, Moderna, Abbott, Arista, Mobileye, McCormick, and More Movers
feed.summary: Stocks were on the rise Thursday following President Donald Trump's announcement of a "framework of a future deal" with NATO concerning Greenland. Equities maintained their gains after new data revealed that the Federal Reserve's preferred inflation gauge largely met expectations in November. The article lists Intel, GE Aerospace, Moderna, Abbott, Arista Networks, Mobileye, and McCormick as some of the day's movers.

feed.title: Is GM Set for 14th Straight EPS Beat in Q4? How to Play the Stock Now
feed.summary: General Motors (GM) is expected to report its fourth-quarter 2025 earnings on January 27, with analysts predicting a 14th consecutive EPS beat. Despite a year-over-year decline in U.S. vehicle deliveries and EV sales in Q4 2025, GM maintained its position as the top-selling automaker in the U.S. for the full year and achieved profitability in China. The company's strategic shift away from high-volume EV production, focus on high-margin vehicles, growing software business, and strong fundamentals suggest potential for further stock upside despite recording $6 billion in special charges for EV rollback.

feed.title: Why Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) Stock Is Up Today
feed.summary: Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) shares rose 4.1% following DB Life Insurance's selection of HPE to build a scalable, sovereign AI foundation for innovation, and NWN's launch of AI-native network services using HPE's Mist technology. Despite this positive news, the stock's gain tempered to 1.9% by afternoon, and HPE's stock has been volatile, previously dropping due to escalating geopolitical tensions. The company's stock is down 11% year-to-date and 18% below its 52-week high.

feed.title: eGain Announces Solve 26 London: Premier Conference on AI-Powered Knowledge Management for Customer Experience Automation
feed.summary: eGain Corporation announced its Solve 26 London conference, a premier event focusing on AI-powered knowledge management for customer experience automation. The conference, scheduled for May 6-7, 2026, aims to address the "AI productivity paradox" by showcasing how trusted, AI-powered knowledge can drive measurable business outcomes. Attendees will hear customer success stories from Global 2000 companies, learn about eGain's product innovations, and participate in hands-on workshops.

feed.title: Genesco Amends Credit Agreement to Extend Maturity, Cut Costs
feed.summary: Genesco (GCO) announced a fourth amendment to its credit agreement, extending the maturity of its revolving credit facility to January 16, 2031, while maintaining current borrowing bases and collateral. This amendment relaxes financial covenants, shifts Canadian rates to Term CORRA, removes credit spread adjustments for domestic borrowings, and revises pricing, aiming to improve funding flexibility and lower financing costs. Spark's AI Analyst gives GCO a "Neutral" rating, citing strong revenue growth and free cash flow balanced by weak profitability.

feed.title: Live Oak signals momentum with 17% loan growth and targets $1B Live Oak Express production over time
feed.summary: Live Oak Bank is demonstrating strong momentum with a 17% loan growth and an ambitious target of $1 billion in Live Oak Express production in the future. This growth indicates a positive outlook for the bank, particularly in its express loan offerings, underscoring its strategic expansion efforts.
